review_notes: The functions are applicable as they relate to audit logging and alerting, which are relevant to the control SI-4 (12). The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern but required slight adjustments for clarity and consistency. The functions map well to Kubernetes security controls, particularly in the context of audit logging and admission control mechanisms.
naming_improvements: Ensure that function names clearly reflect the configuration aspect, such as using 'configured' instead of 'enabled' when appropriate.
title: System Monitoring | Automated Organization-generated Alerts
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4 (12) focuses on automated alerts for system monitoring, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through audit logging and alerting mechanisms. Kubernetes audit logs can be configured to capture significant events, and alerting can be set up using external monitoring tools integrated with Kubernetes. Admission webhooks can also be used to enforce policies and generate alerts on policy violations.
